Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Okay lets try to figure out how to model this process tree based on the given event log So the event log has three cases Let me look at the activities for each case Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hmm so the main sequence seems to be RA then VF then AE Then sometimes theres a loop where RMI is done followed by VF again In case 1 after AE they do RMI then VF then continue to AD In case 3 they do RMI twice then VF then AD So it looks like after AE there might be a possibility to request missing info RMI which requires going back to validate the form VF and this can happen multiple times before moving on to AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the loop probably involves RMI and VF Let me see In the process tree operators the loop is defined as A B which means the process tree A is executed then either you exit the loop or you execute B and then A again this can happen several times until the loop is exited

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: So for example if A is RMI and B is VF then the loop would be RMI then either exit or VF followed by RMI But in the logs after RMI they do VF and then move on to AD not loop back to RMI Hmm maybe thats not the right way

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: Alternatively maybe the loop is structured as VF RMI Lets see So first VF is executed Then either exit the loop or do RMI followed by VF again But in the main flow VF comes before AE so that might not fit

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Wait lets map out the activities in order for each case Case 1 1 RA 2 VF 3 AE 4 RMI 5 VF 6 AD 7 SC 8 AC Case 2 1 RA 2 VF 3 AE 4 AD 5 SC 6 AC Case 3 1 RA 2 VF 3 AE 4 RMI 5 RMI 6 VF 7 AD 8 SC 9 AC So in all cases the sequence starts with RA then VF then AE Then in some cases theres one or more RMI followed by VF then AD SC AC

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after AE the process can do RMI one or more times then VF then proceed In case 3 AE RMI RMI VF So two RMI followed by VF So the loop might allow for multiple RMI steps before VF Alternatively the loop is structured such that after AE you can have a loop where you do RMI and then VF but perhaps RMI can be repeated

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Perhaps the loop is RMI tau but tau is silent Alternatively if the loop is RMI RMI then RMI then either exit or RMI followed by RMI Which would allow for multiple RMI But then after the loop they do VF

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: This is getting a bit confusing Lets think of the loop as a structure where the first part is the main activity and the second part is the redo For example in some processes you have a loop where you do A then if something is wrong you do B and then A again So for example in the case of form validation you validate the form VF then if its okay proceed if not request missing info RMI and then validate again VF

Name: Validation - NE
Text: But in the event logs after AE they do RMI first then VF So VF is after AE So perhaps the loop is after AE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After AE we have a XOR choice between AD and SEQUENCE RMI LOOP RMI tau VF AD

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Alternative approach the process tree should capture that after AE there can be zero or more occurrences of RMI followed by VF and then AD So the structure after AE is RMI VF but how to model a loop that allows for multiple iterations of RMI followed by VF The loop operator A B is such that A is executed then either exit or B followed by A

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: In process mining a common pattern for rework is a loop that allows for a sub-process to be repeated For example after an activity you can either proceed or do a rework step and then repeat the previous activity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The structure after AE is XOR AD SEQUENCE RMI RMI RMI VF AD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 AE AD Case 1 AE RMI VF AD Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF AD Yes this works So the process tree after AE is a XOR with two children 1 AD 2 SEQUENCE of RMI LOOP RMI RMI VF AD
